translation: We have indeed bestowed it [the Qur’aan] in the Night of Destiny.
And what do you understand what the Night of Destiny is?

arabic: لَيۡلَةُ ٱلۡقَدۡرِ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡ أَلۡفِ شَهۡرٖ
transliteration: Laylatu alqadri khayrun min alfi shahrin
translation: The Night of Destiny is better than a thousand months.

arabic: تَنَزَّلُ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ فِيهَا بِإِذۡنِ رَبِّهِم مِّن كُلِّ أَمۡرٖ
transliteration: Tanazzalu almalaikatu waalrroohu feeha biithni rabbihim min kulli amrin
translation: The angels and the Spirit descend in that Night, with their Lord’s decree on every matter,
notes: See study notes 113 to 116 on Verse 2:87 (PART I of this Tafsir) in this regard.

translation: In complete confidence and security. This continues until the break of dawn.
notes: Laylatu alqadri – the night that is described in this 97th Chapter of the Qur’aan – is one of the mutashabihaat (parts of the Holy Book that the human mind cannot clearly understand) in terms of Verse 3:7. Therefore, in pursuance of divine directions in that Verse, we must content ourselves with what Allah Almighty was pleased to inform us about it in this Chapter. But we are not content! We insist on giving our own error-prone interpretations thereof or on searching for such interpretations in the books of ahaadeeth. We are thus guilty of seeking mischief as the Verse 3:7 tells us. According to that Verse only Allah knows its correct interpretation.
